workflow.name: Classify SES project emails with GPT-4.1 mini and save from Gmail to Sheets
workflow.description:
## What this workflow does
This workflow monitors your Gmail inbox every minute and automatically extracts SES (System Engineering Service) project information from recruitment emails. It uses AI to classify emails and parse structured data, then appends project details to a Google Spreadsheet.

## Workflow steps
1. Gmail Trigger polls your inbox every minute
2. AI Project Classifier analyzes each email to determine if it's a project recruitment email
3. Parse JSON converts the AI response into structured data
4. Is Project Email? routes project emails forward; others are skipped
5. Set Spreadsheet Config sets the target spreadsheet ID and sheet name
6. Append to Spreadsheet writes the extracted project data as a new row

## Requirements
- Gmail OAuth2 credentials
- OpenAI API credentials
- Google Sheets OAuth2 credentials
- A Google Spreadsheet with a sheet named `Projects` (or update the sheet name in the Set Spreadsheet Config node)

workflow.name: Save SES project emails from Gmail to Google Sheets

values.content:
You are a sales assistant at an SES (System Engineering Service) company.
Analyze the received email and determine whether it is a project recruitment email (engineer recruitment, job posting, or project introduction).

Criteria for classifying as a project recruitment email:
- Engineer recruitment or introduction
- Development project introduction
- Freelance / SES contract project
- Content seeking specific skills or experience
- Mentions of rates or compensation

Respond ONLY in JSON format. No preamble or explanation needed.

Output format:
{
  "is_project": true/false,
  "project_name": "Project name (use 'Unknown' if unclear)",
  "category": "App / Infrastructure / Unknown (if determinable)",
  "details": "Project overview and details",
  "client_company": "Client company (use 'Unknown' if unclear)",
  "rate": "Rate (10k JPY units) (e.g. 70~80, use 'Unknown' if unclear)",
  "remote": "Remote available / No remote / Partial remote / Unknown",
  "location": "Work location (use 'Unknown' if unclear)",
  "working_hours": "Working hours (e.g. 09:00~18:00, use 'Unknown' if unclear)",
  "required_skills": "Required skills (comma-separated)",
  "preferred_skills": "Nice-to-have skills (comma-separated)",
  "notes": "Other notes"
}:

parameters.content:
## SES Project Management — Gmail → Spreadsheet Auto-Entry

**What this workflow does:**
This workflow monitors your Gmail inbox every minute and automatically extracts SES (System Engineering Service) project information from recruitment emails. It uses AI to classify emails and parse structured data, then appends project details to a Google Spreadsheet.

**Workflow steps:**
1. Gmail Trigger polls your inbox every minute
2. AI Project Classifier analyzes each email to determine if it's a project recruitment email
3. Parse JSON converts the AI response into structured data
4. Is Project Email? routes project emails forward; others are skipped
5. Set Spreadsheet Config sets the target spreadsheet ID and sheet name
6. Append to Spreadsheet writes the extracted project data as a new row

**Requirements:**
- Gmail OAuth2 credentials
- OpenAI API credentials
- Google Sheets OAuth2 credentials
- A Google Spreadsheet with a sheet named `Projects` (or update the sheet name in the Set Spreadsheet Config node)

**Gmail Trigger**
Polls inbox every minute.
Adjust the poll interval or add filters (e.g. label, sender) to reduce noise.

**AI Project Classifier**
Uses GPT-4.1-mini to read the email subject and body and return structured JSON indicating whether the email is a project recruitment post, along with extracted fields (rate, skills, location, etc.).

**Set Spreadsheet Config**
⚙️ Configure here:
- `Spreadsheet ID`: Replace with your Google Spreadsheet ID (found in the sheet URL)
- `Sheet Name`: Name of the target sheet tab (default: `Projects`)

**Append to Spreadsheet**
Writes one row per project email with these columns:
`project_name`, `category`, `details`, `client_company`, `rate`, `remote`, `location`, `working_hours`, `required_skills`, `preferred_skills`, `notes`

Make sure your spreadsheet has these column headers in the first row.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
